triage_reasoning: IZUMO4 (Q1ZYL8) is an IZUMO family member but diverges sharply from its paralogs IZUMO1/2/3 in localization. The NCBI/Alliance annotation explicitly places it in the nucleus. Unlike IZUMO1, which is a classic single-pass TM protein on sperm, IZUMO4 lacks a predicted TM domain and signal peptide consistent with surface display; structural bioinformatics and the available literature describe it as a nuclear-localized protein. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no evidence of stress/ICD-induced surface translocation; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no sperm-surface or other surface display documented even in restricted lineages; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — no lysosomal/endosomal TM topology reported; (4) dual_localization — no PM pool described; (5) stable_surface_attachment — it is not a secreted protein captured at the surface. No antibody/ADC/CAR-T surface-targeting program is documented. The dominant evidence points to a nuclear-resident protein with no membrane anchor.

executive_summary.one_paragraph: IZUMO4 is a soluble, secreted member of the IZUMO immunoglobulin-like family that lacks a transmembrane domain, distinguishing it sharply from its paralogs IZUMO1/2/3. Multiple convergent review and computational sources (a1_evi_01, a1_evi_02, a1_evi_03) describe it as constitutively secreted rather than membrane-anchored, with expression detected in testis round spermatids at the RNA level (a1_evi_05). There is no experimental surface-display evidence and no documented plasma membrane anchor. IZUMO4 is not a viable surface-targeting candidate under current evidence.
executive_summary.accessibility_context_summary: IZUMO4 is a constitutively secreted soluble protein with no membrane anchor; no surface-accessible state has been documented in any tissue or cell type.

filters.expression_level_rationale: Expression detected by scRNA-seq as a round spermatid marker in testis (a1_evi_05); no broad or high-level expression data in the ledger. Restricted germ-cell RNA signal; protein-level evidence absent.
filters.expression_breadth_rationale: Ledger evidence localizes IZUMO4 expression to testis, specifically round spermatids (a1_evi_05); review text notes testis and other tissues broadly (a1_evi_03) but without quantitative data. Overall breadth appears rare/restricted.
filters.surface_specificity_rationale: IZUMO4 is a secreted soluble protein with no TM domain (a1_evi_01, a1_evi_02, a1_evi_03); it is not plasma-membrane resident. The dominant compartment is extracellular/secreted, and no PM pool has been described.
filters.has_known_ligand_rationale: IZUMO4 is a poorly characterized secreted IZUMO family member; no validated endogenous receptor or binding partner has been reported in the literature surveyed. Functional interaction context is limited to computational sperm-egg fusion network studies (a1_evi_04).
filters.low_endogenous_expression_rationale: Derived from expression_level='low' (∈ {low, absent} → flagged). Expression detected by scRNA-seq as a round spermatid marker in testis (a1_evi_05); no broad or high-level expression data in the ledger. Restricted germ-cell RNA signal; protein-level evidence absent.
filters.overexpression_surface_localization_obse: No method observation pairs an overexpression/mixed expression system with a direct or supportive surface-accessibility readout.

surface_evidence.grade_rationale: No direct surface assay (live flow cytometry, nonperm IF, surface biotinylation, or IHC-membranous) exists for IZUMO4 in this ledger. All topology claims are review-level or computational: three moderate-weight claims (a1_evi_01, 02, 03) consistently characterize IZUMO4 as a secreted/soluble protein lacking a transmembrane domain, directly contradicting stable plasma membrane surface display. One computational interaction study (a1_evi_04) is tangential, and one scRNA-seq observation (a1_evi_05) is expression-only. The absence of any direct surface methodology and the convergent evidence for a soluble topology yields a grade of 'weak' — the evidence is consistent but rests entirely on review assertions and computational predictions rather than experimental surface assays.

claim_stances.note: Review assertion: IZUMO4 lacks TM domain unlike IZUMO1/2/3; consistent across family topology description
Computational prediction classifies IZUMO4 as secreted; no direct assay but corroborates a1_evi_01
Review-level characterization as soluble protein lacking TM anchor; no direct surface assay
Structural region definition (C18-H232) used in computational interaction study; no surface assay
scRNA-seq marker in buffalo round spermatids; RNA-level only, no surface assay

expression_observations.context: Testis and other tissues; IZUMO4 described as constitutively soluble protein lacking a TM domain

contradicting_evidence.claim: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is computationally predicted to be a secreted protein, in contrast to the other IZUMO family members (IZUMO1/2/3) which are single-pass membrane proteins. Multiple sources consistently describe IZUMO4 as a constitutively soluble protein expressed in testis and other tissues, with no evidence of a membrane-anchored precursor or sheddase-mediated release. This strongly refutes stable plasma membrane surface display for IZUMO4.
contradicting_evidence.likely_explanation: IZUMO4 appears to be an evolutionarily diverged family member that retained the IZUMO immunoglobulin-like fold but lost the transmembrane segment, rendering it constitutively soluble/secreted. Any apparent 'surface' signal would likely reflect transient association of extracellular soluble protein with the cell surface rather than bona fide membrane anchoring.

structure.source: AlphaFold DB (whole-protein pLDDT — no ECD residues in topology)

surface_bind.attribution: © Balbi et al., Correia lab — EPFL / Inria / Novo Nordisk

co_receptor_requirements.rationale: IZUMO4 is constitutively secreted and lacks a TM domain; it does not reach the plasma membrane and therefore has no surface-expression co-receptor dependency. The question is moot given the secreted topology.

restricted_subdomain.rationale: No surface membrane subdomain localization is reported; the protein is secreted and not membrane-anchored, making subdomain restriction a non-applicable concern. No relevant distribution data in the ledger.

ecd_size_assessment.rationale: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain (a1_evi_01, a1_evi_02) and is constitutively secreted; there is no membrane-tethered extracellular domain by definition. ECD size as an antibody-engagement metric does not apply.

epitope_masking.rationale: No surface-displayed form exists on which to assess epitope masking. The protein is entirely soluble/secreted. Not applicable.

evidence.claim: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain, distinguishing it from the other IZUMO family members (IZUMO1/2/3) that are single-pass membrane proteins. This topology finding directly refutes canonical plasma membrane anchoring for IZUMO4 and supports a secreted/soluble rather than membrane-anchored surface model.

spans.quote: Notably, IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain [ 39 ].

evidence.claim: Computational prediction classifies IZUMO4 as a secreted protein, in contrast to IZUMO1/2/3 which are predicted single-pass membrane proteins. This supports IZUMO4 being a soluble/secreted form rather than a plasma-membrane-anchored surface protein, directly refuting stable surface display.

spans.quote: IZUMO2 and IZUMO3 are predicted to be single-pass membrane proteins like IZUMO1, whereas IZUMO4 appears to be secreted.

evidence.claim: IZUMO4 is described as a soluble protein expressed in the testis and other tissues, indicating it is not membrane-anchored. This refutes plasma membrane surface accessibility and flags IZUMO4 as a secreted/soluble form; the soluble form is the dominant species. No sheddase is implicated — the protein appears constitutively soluble by virtue of lacking a TM domain rather than being shed from a membrane-anchored precursor.

spans.quote: IZUMO4 is a soluble protein expressed in the testis and other tissues.

evidence.claim: In a deep-learning structural study of the sperm-egg fusion synapse, the IZUMO4 region used for binary interaction predictions is defined as C18-H232 (UniProt Q1ZYL8). This region definition is consistent with the entire protein being extracellular/secreted (no intracellular domain segment specified), supporting the interpretation that IZUMO4's folded domain is fully outside the membrane, though the protein itself lacks a TM anchor.

spans.quote: The human protein regions used for the binary interaction predictions whose network is shown in Figure 4 were CD9 P2-V228 (UniProt P21926 ); CD81 M1-Y236 ( P60033 ); DCST1 M1-G706 ( Q5T197 ); DCST2 M1-K773 ( Q5T1A1 ); FIMP A22-S77 (Q96LL3-2); JUNO G20-S228 ( A6ND01 ); IZUMO1 C22-Q284 ( Q8IYV9 ); IZUMO2 C21-P183 ( Q6UXV1 ); IZUMO3 C21-D166 ( Q5VZ72 ); IZUMO4 C18-H232 ( Q1ZYL8 ); MAIA Q16-L580 ( Q96P31 ); SOF1 S29-H122 ( Q96L11 ); SPACA6 C27-T291 ( W5XKT8 ); TMEM81 I31-P218 ( Q6P7N7 ); TMEM95 C17-D140 ( Q3KNT9 ).

evidence.claim: Single-cell RNA-seq of buffalo testis identifies IZUMO4 as a marker gene for the round spermatid stage of spermatogenesis (DNAH14+/IZUMO4+/NKAPL+). This is an RNA-level expression observation in germ cells with no accompanying surface-method validation; it qualifies IZUMO4 as expressed in round spermatids but does not establish plasma membrane accessibility.

spans.quote: PTPRC + / NKG7 + / KLRF1 + / CD94 + ), undifferentiated spermatogonia (Undiff SPG, SALL4 + / ZBTB16 + / ELAVL2 + ), spermatogonia (SPG, NR6A1 + / FGFR3 + / FMR1 + ), early-stage primary spermatocyte (from preleptotene to zygotene) (Early SPC, TEX12 + / SMCHD1 + / PRSS50 + ), late-stage spermatocyte (from pachytene to secondary spermatocyte) (Late SPC, SYCE2 + / BOLL + / MLH1 + ), round spermatid (RS, DNAH14 + / IZUMO4 + / NKAPL + ), elongating/elongated spermatid (ES, TEX29 + / FBXO24 + / ACRV1 + ), and spermatozoa (Sperm, ODF1 + / ODF2 + / OAZ3 + / AKAP4 + ) ( Figures 1 E, S1 C, and S1D).

query.intent: Baseline curated PMID list for IZUMO4 — establishes the full publication corpus before any targeted retrieval.

Catch any verdict-shifting recent papers on IZUMO4 using new vocabulary (moonlighting, ectopic surface, sperm-surface) that method-anchored queries would miss; critical given the triage prior is nuclear but IZUMO family biology raises sperm-surface possibilities.
Hunt for any stress-induced, cell-state-induced, or shedding-related surface presentation of IZUMO4 — addresses the key_uncertainty that a sperm-surface pool cannot be fully excluded.

Surface topology and structural characterization papers for IZUMO4; needed to confirm or refute the nuclear annotation vs. secreted/surface possibility given UniProt lists a signal peptide (aa 1-15) contradicting DeepTMHMM's zero-TM/zero-SP result.
IZUMO4-specific flow cytometry or surface expression papers, including sperm-surface staining assays where IZUMO family members are classically studied.
IHC/IF evidence for IZUMO4 localization in testis/sperm tissue — tissue-restricted surface expression in sperm is the main contextual uncertainty to resolve.
Retrieve flow cytometry surface-staining papers for IZUMO4; sperm-surface flow assays are the primary direct evidence type for IZUMO family members.
Retrieve immunofluorescence papers for IZUMO4; non-permeabilized IF on sperm cells would be the key evidence for surface vs. intracellular localization.
Retrieve IHC papers for IZUMO4; testis/sperm tissue sections would reveal whether protein is on sperm plasma membrane or intracellular/nuclear.
Retrieve surface biotinylation papers for IZUMO4; would directly confirm or refute surface accessibility on sperm or other cell types.
Retrieve surfaceome/CSC mass-spec papers that may have detected IZUMO4 peptides in surface fractions from sperm or other cells.
Retrieve paired surface-fraction + total-lysate western blot papers; would disambiguate secreted vs. surface-retained vs. intracellular pool.
Retrieve structural studies of IZUMO4 or the IZUMO domain family; AlphaFold model exists (Q1ZYL8) and may inform whether the protein adopts a secreted/surface-compatible fold.

Catch sperm-egg fusion assays, soluble IZUMO4 detection in seminal fluid, or proximity-labeling studies — the functional assay idiom for secreted/sperm-surface proteins that don't fit the 7 method buckets.
Host-agnostic OE query: IZUMO4 AND (overexpression OR transfected OR stable cell line) AND (cell surface OR flow cytometry) — catches any heterologous expression system surface-localization paper.
HEK293/HEK293T OE surface query for IZUMO4; most common heterologous host for recombinant expression of secreted/surface proteins.

IZUMO family context query — search for IZUMO family (IZUMO1/2/3/4) surface localization papers that may include IZUMO4 data or cross-reactivity context; family-level surface biology informs paralog-based inference.
Fetch the SPDI paper (UniProt top publication) — may contain bioinformatic surface/secreted classification data for IZUMO4 that informs topology debate.

Nuclear vs. secreted localization debate for IZUMO4 — target papers that experimentally address whether IZUMO4 reaches the secretory pathway or remains nuclear/cytoplasmic, resolving the contradiction between UniProt signal peptide annotation and triage nuclear verdict.
Baseline curated PMID list for IZUMO4 to seed the biology ledger
Recent publications mentioning IZUMO4 surface/membrane/sperm biology; catch any post-2022 experimental localization reports
HPA antibody panels + IHC literature for IZUMO4 tissue distribution; check for testis/sperm staining pattern and any off-tissue signal
Immunofluorescence localization of IZUMO4 in sperm or other cell types; subcellular compartment calls (nuclear vs membrane)
Flow cytometry surface detection of IZUMO4 on intact sperm or other cells; surface vs intracellular gating evidence
Surface proteomics datasets that may have detected IZUMO4 on sperm surface or other cell types
IZUMO4 subcellular localization and tissue expression; confirm nuclear annotation vs any membrane/surface pool in sperm or testis
Normal tissue tox panel: surface expression of IZUMO4 in liver, lung, kidney, GI tract, heart, brain — establish negative surface reads for tox organs
IZUMO4 secreted/shed form characterization; UniProt lists it as Secreted — assess whether soluble form circulates or is detected in seminal plasma/serum
IZUMO family structural biology; confirm IZUMO4 lacks TM domain and signal peptide consistent with surface display, vs IZUMO1/2/3 topology
SPDI paper describing IZUMO4 as a secreted/TM protein discovery; assess original secreted annotation basis

confidence_reasoning: Confidence is moderate rather than low because multiple independent sources converge on the same conclusion — IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is constitutively secreted — but these sources are all review assertions or computational predictions rather than direct experimental assays (e.g. western blot of conditioned medium, live-cell surface biotinylation, or flow cytometry). The triage verdict of 'nuclear' is partially at odds with the ledger, which describes secreted rather than nuclear localization; both agree there is no plasma membrane surface display. Confidence would rise to high if a direct surface-proteomics or conditioned-medium mass spectrometry study confirmed the secreted topology and ruled out any membrane-anchored isoform, particularly in sperm or testicular cell lines.
